description:
## Component: hx-dialog (NEW) **Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 6/6 libraries** Modal and non-modal overlay dialog for confirmations, forms, and content display. Uses native `` element internally. ### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite) Properties control open state and modal behavior. Slots accept all content from Drupal — headers, body, actions. ### API Design **Properties:** `open` (boolean), `modal` (boolean, default true), `label` (accessible dialog title), `close-on-overlay` (boolean), `close-on-escape` (boolean, default true), `no-close-button` (boolean) **Slots:** `header` (title/heading), default (body content), `footer` (action buttons) **CSS Parts:** `dialog`, `overlay`, `header`, `body`, `footer`, `close-button` **Events:** `hx-open`, `hx-close` (composed: true, detail: { returnValue }), `hx-request-close` (cancelable — prevent close) ### Drupal Integration ```twig {# Confirmation dialog — Drupal controls all content #}

Delete Patient Record?

This action cannot be undone. All associated records will be permanently removed.

Patient: {{ patient.label }} (MRN: {{ patient.field_mrn.value }})

Cancel Delete Permanently
{# Form inside dialog — Drupal Form API #}

Add Clinical Note

{{ form.form_build_id }} {{ form.form_token }} Note
Save Note
``` All content is slot-driven — Drupal controls header text, body content (including forms, alerts, rich HTML), and footer actions. Critical for CMS integration: forms inside dialogs must work with Drupal Form API. ### Healthcare: Deletion confirmations, clinical notes, appointment scheduling, consent forms ### Acceptance Criteria 1. Uses native `` element (top-layer, no z-index battles) 2. Modal mode: overlay backdrop, focus trap, scroll lock 3. Non-modal mode: no overlay, no focus trap 4. Focus trap cycles through focusable elements 5. Return focus to trigger element on close 6. Escape key closes (configurable) 7. Close on overlay click (configurable) 8. WCAG 2.1 AA: dialog role, aria-labelledby, focus management 9. Forms inside dialog work with Drupal Form API 10. Smooth open/close animation
title: T1-27: hx-dialog — Modal/Non-Modal Dialog

Critical for CMS integration: forms inside dialogs must work with Drupal Form API. ### Healthcare: Deletion confirmations, clinical notes, appointment scheduling, consent forms ### Acceptance Criteria 1. lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 5**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-dialog/hx-dialog.styles.ts`:
- Around line 12-183: The stylesheet uses hardcoded values that must be replaced
with component-level design tokens: replace literal color `#0f172a` in dialog and
.dialog__panel with a token (e.g. --hx-dialog-color), replace the box-shadow 0
20px 60px rgba(...) with a token (e.g. --hx-dialog-shadow), swap spacing/size
literals (0.75rem, 1rem, 1.5rem, 2rem) used in .dialog__header, .dialog__label,
.dialog__body, .dialog__close-button, .dialog__footer with semantic component
tokens (e.g. --hx-dialog-space-3, --hx-dialog-space-4, --hx-dialog-space-6,
--hx-dialog-space-8), and replace timing/opacity hardcodes (150ms, 200ms, 0.6)
in .dialog__close-button and `@keyframes` dialog-in with tokens like
--hx-transition-fast, --hx-transition-normal, and --hx-dialog-close-opacity;
update all referenced selectors (dialog, .dialog__panel, .dialog__header,
.dialog__label, .dialog__body, .dialog__footer, .dialog__close-button,
dialog::backdrop, `@keyframes` dialog-in) to consume these tokens with semantic
fallbacks so no literal values remain.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-dialog/hx-dialog.test.ts`:
- Line 289: Remove the trailing non-null assertions from DOM queries (e.g., the
declaration using shadowQuery<HTMLButtonElement>(el, '[part="close-button"]')!)
and instead explicitly assert existence before use: store the query result in a
nullable variable (e.g., closeBtn), add an existence check (either
expect(closeBtn).not.toBeNull() in tests or an if (!closeBtn) throw Error/ fail
the test) to narrow the type, then proceed using the now-narrowed variable;
apply the same pattern to the other occurrences mentioned (lines referencing
shadowQuery results at 301, 316, 327, 425, 433, 469,
